title: Centropogon costaricae

This family, commonly known as the melastome family, comprises approximately 165 genera and 5,000 species of flowering plants. intro: Centropogon costaricae is a flowering plant species in the family Pentaphylacaceae, native to Costa Rica and other parts of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. Bioactive constituents of Centropogon costaricae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to aid in respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
